answer:
**The passage appears in *Bang-E-Dara Part 2* (Allama Iqbal), chapter 9 ("Chaand aur Taare").**[^1]

In this poem, the stars complain to the moon about their weariness:

> Fearing the break of day,  
> The stars began to say  
> To the moon: "The sights remain  
> The same on heaven's plain;  
> And we have tired of light,  
> Of shining ever bright."[^2]

The moon replies (verbatim):

> To pause upon this path is but a flaw,  
> For hidden in Stillness is death's own law.[^3]

[^1]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch.9 ("Chaand aur Taare"), [chapter](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/09-chaand-aur-taare/translation/).
[^2]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch.9 ("Chaand aur Taare"), [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/09-chaand-aur-taare/translation/#p-6fe8f3).
[^3]: Allama Iqbal, *Bang-E-Dara Part 2*, ch.9 ("Chaand aur Taare"), [paragraph](/works/allama-iqbal-bang-e-dara-part-2-9bc58c/09-chaand-aur-taare/translation/#p-fb94a9).
